body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 54/2026
Made under: Gaming Control Act
Published in: No. 6 – B.C. Regs. 43 to 56, April 7, 2026
Deposit date: April 7, 2026

B.C. Reg. 54/2026, deposited April 7, 2026, under theGAMING CONTROL ACT[sections 193 and 202]. Order in Council 132/2026, approved and ordered April 7, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Administrator, by and with the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders that, effective April 13, 2026, the Gaming Control Regulation, B.C. Reg. 209/2025, is amended as set out in the attached Schedule. — N. KRIEGER,Minister of Public Safety and Solicitor General; R. PARMAR,Presiding Member of the Executive Council. 1	Section 10 of the Gaming Control Regulation, B.C. Reg. 209/2025, is amended (a)	in paragraph (d) by adding "$1 million or more but" before "less than $5 million", (b)	by adding the following paragraph: (d.1)	the E supplier class, consisting of registered gaming services providers that are authorized to distribute gaming supplies to the lottery corporation for use in online gaming schemes and that are projected to earn from that distribution a gross annual revenue of less than $1 million;, and (c)	in paragraph (e) by striking out "E supplier" and substituting "F supplier". 2	Section 11 is amended in Table 1 (a)	by adding the following item as indicated: Class of Registered Gaming ServicesProvider Established by Section 10 (b)	in item 5 by striking out "E supplier" and substituting "F supplier". Index Page|Previous Regulation|Next Regulation|Cumulative Index Page Copyright © 2026: King's Printer, Victoria, British Columbia, Canada

minutes_briefs.title: New E supplier class for small online gaming distributors in BC
minutes_briefs.summary: British Columbia has created a new 'E supplier' class for registered gaming services providers that distribute gaming supplies to the lottery corporation for online gaming and earn less than $1 million in gross annual revenue from that distribution. The previous 'E supplier' class has been renamed 'F supplier'. This change takes effect April 13, 2026.
minutes_briefs.action_required: If you are a gaming services provider earning under $1 million from online gaming supply distribution, check whether you now qualify for the new E supplier class.

key_dates.label: New E supplier class for small online gaming distributors takes effect
